articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Lionel Messi left on bench as Argentina beat Honduras
article.description: Argentina beat Honduras 2-0 on Saturday in their second-to-last friendly before the FIFA World Cup begins on June 11.
article.linkText: Messi left on bench as Argentina beat Honduras

article.story:
Argentina beat Honduras 2-0 on Saturday in their second-to-last friendly before the FIFA World Cup begins on June 11.

A raucous Kyle Field in College Station, Texas, cheered Argentina loudly throughout the match, but were likely disappointed as the team's talisman Lionel Messi remained on the bench as he recovers from muscle fatigue.

Lautaro Martínez got the scoring going from the penalty spot for the defending World Cup champions in the 37th minute after a clumsy foul on Nicolás Tagliafico earned Argentina a spot kick.

Atlético Madrid attacker Giuliano Simeone scored to double Argentina's lead early in the second half, with Lautaro's clever backheel setting up his teammate for a close-range finish.

"We have to go defend what we achieved in Qatar. It's going to be tough, but we're going to give it everything to leave Argentina as high as possible," Martinez said after the match.

Scaloni made a host of changes soon after Simeone's goal, but Messi was not one of them. The 38-year-old star sustained a minor injury on May 25 while playing his last match with Inter Miami before joining the national team.

"When Argentines step onto a soccer field, it's very hard for them to hold back," Scaloni said with regard to members of his squad who did not play. "They always give everything, they hold nothing back. That's why we manage the workloads and try to give minutes to the majority."

The team has said Messi's recovery time will depend on "his clinical and functional progress."

Messi, who's widely expected to retire from international soccer after this summer's tournament, holds the record for most World Cup matches (26) and needs four more goals to surpass Germany's Miroslav Klose's World Cup record of 16.

Argentina begin play in Group J against Algeria on June 16, before facing Austria and Jordan. Piror to that, Scaloni's side will face Iceland on June 9 in its final friendly before the World Cup.

ESPN's Lizzy Becherano contributed to this recap. Information from The Associated Press was also used.
